title: Centaurium umbellatum
taxonomy: Centaurium umbellatum is a flowering plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia. The species is also known as greater centaury or small centaury. This plant is a perennial herb that grows up to 60 cm in height. The classification of Centaurium umbellatum also includes its genus, Centaurium, which belongs to the order Gentianales.
uses: Centaurium umbellatum, also known as the grassy centaury, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant's extracts are thought to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of various infections. In addition, Centaurium umbellatum has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential side effects.

preparations: Centaurium umbellatum, also known as blue chamomile or lesser balm, is used in herbal preparations due to its potential medicinal properties. intro: Centaurium umbellatum, also known as gentian or common gentian, is a perennial plant belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health. Bioactive constituents of Centaurium umbellatum include xanthones, flavonoids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Centaurium umbellatum is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dry extract, often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, and to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
